mission: Implement canvas offset fix directly in designer.bundle.js

changes_made.purpose: Helper function to calculate CSS padding offset dynamically
features: Automatic mobile/desktop adaptation (responds to CSS media queries)
Error handling with fallback to zero offset

changes_made.purpose: ADD offset when initially saving image coordinates to designer state
changes_made.operation: SAVE - Adds offset to convert canvas-relative to container-relative coordinates
changes_made.backward_compatibility: Metadata flags distinguish new format from old format

changes_made.purpose: ADD offset when updating coordinates during drag/drop operations
changes_made.operation: UPDATE - Adds offset on every 'modified' event (triggered by user dragging logo)
changes_made.critical_path: This is called on EVERY drag/drop - most frequently executed save path

changes_made.purpose: SUBTRACT offset for new designs when loading (reverse of save operation)
changes_made.operation: LOAD - Converts container-relative back to canvas-relative for Fabric.js

backward_compatibility.old_designs: No metadata.offset_applied flag - coordinates used as-is (canvas-relative)
backward_compatibility.new_designs: metadata.offset_applied === true - offset subtracted before applying to Fabric.js
backward_compatibility.detection_method: Check for presence and value of metadata.offset_applied flag

offset_applied.description: True for designs saved after fix, undefined/false for old designs
offset_applied.purpose: Distinguish between old format (canvas-relative) and new format (container-relative)

offset_x.description: X offset value at time of save (typically 50px on desktop, 0px on mobile)
offset_x.purpose: Store actual offset used for precise reversal on load

offset_y.description: Y offset value at time of save (typically 50px on desktop, 0px on mobile)
offset_y.purpose: Store actual offset used for precise reversal on load

offset_fix_version.purpose: Track which version of fix was applied (for future migrations)

offset_fix_timestamp.description: When the design was saved with offset fix

new_designs_load.action: Subtract offset to get canvas-relative coords

mobile.automatic_adaptation: getBoundingClientRect() naturally returns 0px offset when padding is 0

responsive_design_handling.robustness: Dynamic calculation via getBoundingClientRect() automatically adapts to any CSS changes without code modifications

old_designs_behavior.visual_result: Render at same position as before fix

new_designs_behavior.on_load: Offset subtracted before applying to Fabric.js

mixed_environment.scenario: Both old and new designs exist in database
mixed_environment.handling: Each design checked individually via metadata flag

steps: 1. Open designer with existing design (no metadata.offset_applied)
2. Check that logo renders at same visual position as before fix
3. Verify no coordinate shift or visual displacement
testing_needed.expected_result: Old designs render exactly as before (backward compatible)

testing_needed.scenario: Save NEW design with logo placement
steps: 1. Open designer, drag logo to visual Y=200px from editor top
2. Check console for 'OFFSET-FIX: Calculated offset' log
testing_needed.expected_result: New designs save with offset-adjusted coordinates and metadata flags

steps: 1. Save design with logo at visual Y=200px
4. Check console for 'OFFSET-FIX: Loading NEW design - subtracting offset'
5. Verify logo renders at Y=200px (same visual position)
testing_needed.expected_result: New designs load at correct visual position (offset subtracted correctly)

steps: 2. Drag logo to new position (e.g., Y=300px visual)
5. Reload page and verify position maintained at Y=300px visual
testing_needed.expected_result: Drag/drop updates save with correct offset

steps: 3. Check console offset log (should show offsetX: 0, offsetY: 0)
1. Place logo on front view at Y=200px
5. Reload and verify both views load at correct positions
testing_needed.expected_result: Multi-view designs work correctly with offset fix

steps: 1. Place logo on default variation at Y=200px
3. Verify logo copied to new variation
4. Check that both variations have offset metadata
5. Load both variations and verify correct positioning
testing_needed.expected_result: Multi-variation designs work correctly (offset copied to all variations)

steps: 5. Reload and verify both logos at correct positions
testing_needed.expected_result: Multiple images per view all handled correctly

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Bundle edits overwritten by future webpack rebuild
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Document all changes clearly. Consider recreating webpack source files and config. Keep backup files. Add comment markers (🔧 OFFSET-FIX) for easy identification.
risks_and_mitigations.action_required: Create webpack source files from decompiled bundle OR document fix for future rebuilds

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Old designs not rendering correctly after fix
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Metadata flag detection ensures old designs (without offset_applied flag) use coordinates as-is. Extensive backward compatibility logic implemented.
risks_and_mitigations.action_required: Test with real production data before full deployment

risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: getBoundingClientRect() automatically adapts to CSS media queries. When padding is 0 on mobile, offset calculation naturally returns {x:0, y:0}.
risks_and_mitigations.action_required: Test on actual mobile devices and various screen sizes

risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: getBoundingClientRect() is extremely fast (<1ms). Only called on save/load operations, not on every render frame.
Need to verify that PHP backend saves metadata field when persisting design data. If metadata is stripped, backward compatibility detection won't work.
risks_and_mitigations.action_required: CRITICAL - Agent 4 must verify database persistence of metadata field

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Renderer (PHP backend) not aware of new coordinate format
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: If PHP renderer expects canvas-relative coords, it may render designs incorrectly. Need to verify renderer behavior with new format.
risks_and_mitigations.action_required: CRITICAL - Agent 4 must check PHP renderer and adjust if needed

validation_checklist.item: loadViewImage() patched with backward-compatible offset subtraction

code_quality_measures.error_handling: Comprehensive try-catch in getCanvasOffset() with graceful fallback
code_quality_measures.logging: Console logs with emoji markers for easy filtering (🔧 OFFSET-FIX)
code_quality_measures.code_comments: All modified sections have explanatory comments
code_quality_measures.backward_compatibility: Explicit metadata checks with fallback behavior

critical_tasks: 1. VERIFY DATABASE PERSISTENCE: Check that metadata.offset_applied, offset_x, offset_y are saved to database
2. TEST OLD DESIGNS: Load existing production designs and verify they render unchanged
3. TEST NEW DESIGNS: Save new designs and verify coordinates match visual position
4. TEST DRAG/DROP: Verify updateImageTransform() works correctly on drag operations
5. CHECK PHP RENDERER: Verify backend rendering system works with new coordinate format
6. MOBILE TESTING: Test on mobile devices (width < 720px) to verify 0px offset handling
7. REGRESSION TESTING: Run all test scenarios from 'testing_needed' section
8. PERFORMANCE MONITORING: Check for any performance degradation

database_verification.query_example: SELECT metadata FROM designs WHERE id = X
database_verification.old_designs: Should have metadata = null or undefined
database_verification.new_designs: Should have complete metadata object with all flags

php_renderer_check.concern: PHP backend may not be aware of new coordinate format
php_renderer_check.location_to_check: Search for files that read imageData.transform.left/top for rendering
php_renderer_check.potential_issue: If renderer expects canvas-relative coords, may need adjustment
php_renderer_check.solution: Either: (a) Teach renderer about metadata.offset_applied flag, OR (b) Store both formats

if_critical_issues_found: 3. Verify system reverts to pre-fix behavior

success_criteria: ✓ Old designs render at same visual position as before
✓ New designs save coordinates that match visual position
✓ PHP renderer works with new format (or adjusted)

implementation_summary.approach: Direct bundle editing (no webpack source available)

all_modified_lines: Line 1111: loadViewImage() backward-compatible offset handling (fromURL path)
Line 1135: loadViewImage() backward-compatible offset handling (existing fabric path)

deviations.planned: Inline offset calculation in each function

deviations.implemented: Enhanced metadata with version and timestamp
deviations.reason: Better audit trail and future migration support
